question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a startTime set to a past consensus timestamp of a known fee-schedule topic message, causing poll() to re-deliver already-processed fee modification messages to downstream consumers, leading to double-application of transaction fee changes outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user submit a TopicMessageFilter with limit=1 and a startTime aligned to a fee-schedule update message, causing the (int) cast of (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) to produce an unexpected page size, resulting in partial delivery of fee modification records that leaves the fee state inconsistently applied?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged user open an unbounded number of concurrent gRPC subscriptions via listen() with filters targeting a fee-schedule topic, exhausting the boundedElastic() scheduler thread pool and causing fee-update messages to be delayed or dropped, effectively freezing transaction fees at stale values outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user set filter.getLimit() to Long.MAX_VALUE so that the expression (int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) overflows to a negative integer, causing Math.min(limit, listenerProperties.getMaxPageSize()) to return a negative pageSize, which triggers an error in topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() that silently skips fee-update messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged user exploit a race condition between onNext() setting last via last.set(topicMessage) and getLast() reading it in poll(), causing the startTime calculation (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) to use a stale or wrong timestamp, resulting in fee-update messages being skipped or re-polled outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user supply a TopicMessageFilter with startTime equal to Long.MAX_VALUE - 1, causing last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 to overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE, making the newFilter.startTime wrap around and poll all historical fee-schedule messages from the beginning of time, leading to unintended fee state replay?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a fee-schedule topic with RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) effectively creating an infinite polling loop, and by doing so at scale cause the topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() to be called at maximum frequency, creating a database query storm that delays legitimate fee-update propagation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user set filter.getLimit() to a value just above context.getCount().get() so that the computed pageSize becomes 1, causing poll() to retrieve fee-update messages one at a time per interval cycle, artificially slowing fee-schedule propagation and leaving the system operating on outdated transaction fees for extended periods?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged user manipulate the TopicMessageFilter.subscriberId field (randomly generated via RandomStringUtils) to collide with an existing subscriber's ID, causing the PollingContext to be shared or confused, leading to incorrect count and last tracking that skips fee-update messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user craft a filter with endTime set just before a known fee-schedule update consensus timestamp, causing poll() to build a newFilter that excludes that fee-update message, effectively hiding a transaction fee modification from downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged user open a subscription with a very small interval (at the minimum 50ms boundary from ListenerProperties) targeting a fee-schedule topic, causing poll() to be called at maximum rate and flooding topicMessageRepository with findByFilter() calls, degrading database performance and delaying fee-update delivery to other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user set filter.limit to exactly context.getCount().get() so that (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) equals zero, causing pageSize to be zero, and triggering topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() with a zero-limit query that returns all remaining fee-update messages unbounded, bypassing the maxPageSize design constraint?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where onNext() is called concurrently from multiple threads (due to boundedElastic() scheduler), causing count.incrementAndGet() and last.set() to be out of sync, resulting in poll() computing an incorrect remaining limit that causes fee-update messages to be fetched beyond the intended limit?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a fee-schedule topic with no limit (limit=0, hasLimit()=false), causing poll() to use Integer.MAX_VALUE as the limit and Math.min(Integer.MAX_VALUE, maxPageSize) to always return maxPageSize, and by doing so at scale exhaust database connection pools and prevent fee-update messages from being written or read?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user supply a TopicMessageFilter targeting a fee-schedule topic with startTime set to 0 (minimum allowed by @Min(0)), causing poll() to query all fee-update messages from the genesis of the ledger, creating an extremely large result set from topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() that causes memory exhaustion and disrupts fee-update processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the jitter(0.1) in RepeatSpec to cause polling intervals to cluster at the minimum boundary, effectively increasing poll frequency beyond the configured interval and causing topicMessageRepository to be queried more often than designed, impacting fee-update message ordering guarantees?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a topicId corresponding to a fee-schedule governance topic and a startTime just before a fee-update message, then immediately cancel and re-subscribe, causing the PollingContext to reset and re-deliver the fee-update message, leading to duplicate fee modification processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged user open thousands of simultaneous listen() subscriptions on a fee-schedule topic, each creating a new PollingContext and Flux pipeline, exhausting the boundedElastic() scheduler's thread pool and causing legitimate fee-update subscribers to be starved of scheduler time, delaying fee modifications?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.fromStream() call in poll() by causing topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() to return a Java Stream that is never closed (stream leak), and by doing so at scale hold database cursors open indefinitely, preventing fee-update messages from being committed and visible to other pollers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user set filter.getLimit() to a value such that (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) is negative due to count exceeding limit (possible if onNext() is called more times than limit allows), causing Math.min() to receive a negative value and pageSize to be negative, leading to undefined behavior in findByFilter() that corrupts fee-update message delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a fee-schedule topic and then hold the gRPC stream open indefinitely without consuming messages, causing backpressure to build up in the Flux pipeline and the boundedElastic() scheduler to accumulate pending tasks, eventually starving other fee-update subscribers of processing resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user craft a filter where startTime is set to the exact consensus timestamp of a fee-update message (not +1), causing poll() to re-include that message in every polling cycle (since last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 only advances after onNext() is called), leading to repeated delivery of the same fee modification?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where the Flux pipeline emits a fee-update TopicMessage but onNext() is never called (e.g., due to a downstream error before doOnNext()), causing last to remain null and poll() to always use filter.getStartTime(), resulting in the same fee-update message being polled and delivered repeatedly?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the delaySubscription(interval, scheduler) call to time their subscription precisely so that a fee-update message published just before the delay expires is missed by the first poll(), and by manipulating the subscription timing cause the system to operate on stale fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user supply a TopicMessageFilter with a very large limit (e.g., Integer.MAX_VALUE as a long), causing the (int) cast of (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) to silently truncate, producing a pageSize smaller than intended and causing poll() to miss fee-update messages that fall beyond the truncated page boundary?
